title: Nassella pfisteri
intro: Nassella pfisteri, also known as Purple Needle Grass, is a perennial grass native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which can help relieve congestion and promote mucus clearance. The bioactive constituents of Nassella pfisteri include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. Nassella pfisteri is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and it is also used in cough syrups and expectorants to help alleviate respiratory symptoms.
taxonomy: Nassella pfisteri is classified as a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Nassella, which is a group of grasses native to the Americas. Nassella pfisteri is specifically classified as a member of the subfamily Pooideae and is closely related to other species within the genus. The classification of Nassella pfisteri is further refined to the species level based on its unique morphological characteristics. Nassella pfisteri is a rare and endemic species found in specific regions of its native habitat.
